 Your guide to online safety 
 


 

 
 
 

 

 
  
 
 
 
   
 
 
 

 
 

 
  
 
 How AI personas fuel scams 
 
 AI-generated personas use advanced technology to create fake videos that look way too real. Their goal? To deceive, steal data, or spread malware. Let’s learn how to spot them. 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
   
 
 
 

 Example 1
 
Meet Thomas Harris
 
An AI persona pretends to offer TradingView tips, but instead installs a remote access trojan that steals data.


 
 
 
   
 
 
 

 
 
Example 1
 
Meet Thomas Harris
 
An AI persona pretends to offer TradingView tips, but instead installs a remote access trojan that steals data.

 
 

 
 
  
 
 
 
   
 
 
 

 Example 2
 
Meet Michael, Todd, and Jane
 
AI-generated videos promise quick profits through coding tutorials, but they hide malicious code designed to steal money.


 
 
 
   
 
 
 

 
 
Example 2
 
Meet Michael, Todd, and Jane
 
AI-generated videos promise quick profits through coding tutorials, but they hide malicious code designed to steal money.

How to spot scam videos
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 Question the offer

 
  

 
 
 If it sounds too good to be true or promises easy money for little effort, it probably is.

 
 
 
 

 
  

 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 Check the handle

 
  

 
 
 Look closely at the @handle — even small differences from the real one can mean it’s fake.

 
 
 
 

 
  

 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 Watch for odd details

 
  

 
 
 If the creator seems different or the video doesn’t match their usual vibe, it’s worth a second look.

 
 
 
 

 
  

 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 Read the description

 
  

 
 
 Scam videos often have vague or inconsistent descriptions that don’t match the channel’s usual tone.

 
 
 
 

 
  

 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 Don’t run code or commands

 
  

 
 
 Never follow tech instructions unless you’re absolutely sure the source is legitimate.

 Spot the scam videos: What not to do 
 
  
 
 

 
 
 
  

 
 
 
  
 
  
 
 
 
 
 Check if the offer sounds too good to be true.

 
  

 
 
 
 
  
 
 

 
  

 
 
 
  
 
  
 
 
 
 
 Verify the channel’s handle and watch for odd details.
